name: Hadoop 1.x (Classic) Architecture
description:
The Classic Master-Slave Distributed Framework

Hadoop 1.x represents the foundational architecture for processing vast amounts of data across clusters. It consists of two primary layers: HDFS (Hadoop Distributed File System) for storage and MapReduce for computation.

  • NameNode: The master of HDFS, managing file system metadata and block mapping.
  • JobTracker: The master of MapReduce, responsible for resource management and task scheduling.
  • DataNodes & TaskTrackers: The worker nodes that store data and execute computational logic, respectively.

Note: A key characteristic of Hadoop 1.x is the tight coupling between resource management and the MapReduce programming model, which was later decoupled in Hadoop 2.x via YARN.
